From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

Kenyan long-distance runner

David Ruto

| Medal record | | |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Men's [athletics](/wiki/Athletics_(sport) "Athletics (sport)") | | |
| Representing  [Kenya](/wiki/Kenya "Kenya") | | |
| [IAAF World Half Marathon Championships](/wiki/IAAF_World_Half_Marathon_Championships "IAAF World Half Marathon Championships") | | |
| Gold medal – first place | [2000 Veracruz](/wiki/2000_IAAF_World_Half_Marathon_Championships "2000 IAAF World Half Marathon Championships") | Team |

**David Kiptarus Ruto** (born 1980) is a Kenyan former [long-distance runner](/wiki/Long-distance_runner "Long-distance runner") who competed in [road races](/wiki/Road_running "Road running"). He was the team gold medallist at the [2000 IAAF World Half Marathon Championships](/wiki/2000_IAAF_World_Half_Marathon_Championships "2000 IAAF World Half Marathon Championships") alongside [Paul Tergat](/wiki/Paul_Tergat "Paul Tergat") and [Joseph Kimani](/wiki/Joseph_Kimani "Joseph Kimani"), having finished fifth individually.

Ruto competed from 2000 to 2009 and his biggest win was at the 2003 [Miami Marathon](/wiki/Miami_Marathon "Miami Marathon"). He also had runner-up finishes at the [Three Hearts Marathon](/wiki/Three_Hearts_Marathon "Three Hearts Marathon"), [Brussels Marathon](/wiki/Brussels_Marathon "Brussels Marathon") and [Mumbai Marathon](/wiki/Mumbai_Marathon "Mumbai Marathon"). He ran the marathon in under two hours and ten minutes on two occasions, including 2:09:21 for third at the 2003 [Eindhoven Marathon](/wiki/Eindhoven_Marathon "Eindhoven Marathon") and 2:08:21 hours at the 2003 [Paris Marathon](/wiki/Paris_Marathon "Paris Marathon").[[1]](#cite_note-1)[[2]](#cite_note-2)
